title: What Is Really the Point of All of This?
author: Navees
summary: Spreadsheets, application dates, minimum spends. Why do I bother with any of it? Here's the honest answer.
body_html:
Sometimes I stop and ask myself — what is really the point of all of this?

The spreadsheets. The application dates. Tracking 5/24. Timing minimum spends. It's a lot of admin for a hobby.

Here's the honest answer: it buys experiences I would never, ever pay cash for. A $12,000 Qantas First seat for 110,000 miles. A pre-flight massage in a lounge. Weeks in hotels that would've cost a fortune. I'm not rich — I just play the game the banks set up, and I play it well.

It's not really about the points. Points are just numbers. It's about turning a bit of organisation into the kind of travel most people assume is out of reach.

The point isn't the points. It's the seat, the lounge, the trip you'd never have booked otherwise.

So yeah — it's worth it. Just don't lose the plot and start paying interest or chasing a bonus you'll never use. Keep it fun, keep it profitable, and go somewhere good.
